message:
bd-28t8: Implement autocomplete UI dropdown + selection/insertion

Add interactive autocomplete dropdown to the TUI editor with:

- Trigger conditions: Tab key, or auto-trigger on `/` and `@`
- Navigation: Up/Down arrows to move selection
- Acceptance: Enter/Tab to insert selected item
- Cancellation: Escape to close dropdown
- Visual: Styled dropdown with icons, selection highlight, descriptions

The dropdown displays up to 10 items with scroll indicator for
longer lists. Each item shows a kind icon (⚡ command, 📄 template,
🔧 skill, 📁 file, 📂 path) and optional description for the
selected item.

Also adds const fn annotations to satisfy clippy::missing_const_for_fn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1mbs","title":"Editor: Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment","description":"# Goal
Support image attachment UX in interactive mode:
- Ctrl+V to paste an image from clipboard.
- Drag/drop a file onto the terminal to attach.

# Legacy Behavior
- On Ctrl+V, legacy tries to read clipboard image; if present, it writes to a temp file and inserts the file path at cursor.
  - See `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` (`handleClipboardImagePaste`).

# Required Behavior
- Ctrl+V:
  - If clipboard contains an image, write it to a temp file (png/jpg based on mime if possible).
  - Insert the resulting path into the editor at the cursor.
  - If clipboard has no image or access denied, do nothing silently.

- Drag/drop:
  - Many terminals paste a quoted or escaped file path. Ensure we normalize/sanitize so that subsequent file reference processing can resolve it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can attach an image without manually saving a file.
- [ ] No crashes when clipboard access is unavailable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.385818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:42.357212214Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.395051624Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:42.357181036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.861448590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352770513Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352674283Z","close_reason":"Session picker uses index with fallback","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.870643664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101795250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-28rk","title":"Autocomplete: core provider (commands/prompts/skills/files/paths)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core autocomplete provider that produces suggestions based on editor text + cursor position.

# Suggestions required (legacy parity)
- Slash commands: built-in commands (`/help`, `/model`, `/settings`, etc.).
- Prompt templates: `/<template>` from ResourceLoader.
- Skills: `/skill:<name>` when enableSkillCommands is true.
- File reference: when user types `@`, fuzzy-search project files and insert selected path.
- Path completion: when cursor is in a path-like token, produce candidates (use `fd` if available).

# Matching
- Fuzzy matching for file and command lists.
- Stable ordering (deterministic): exact prefix matches first, then fuzzy score.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep provider logic independent of rendering.
- Provider should return a structured `AutocompleteItem`:
  - display label
  - insert text
  - optional description
  - kind (command/template/skill/file/path)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provider can be called with (text, cursor) and returns stable suggestions.
- [ ] Provider includes file suggestions without scanning the entire filesystem on every keystroke (cache or incremental search).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyHill","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:08.177076040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441887518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441818900Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:08.186267387Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-28uy","title":"Interactive: /copy copies last assistant message to clipboard","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `/copy` behavior: copy last assistant message text to the system clipboard.

# Required Behavior
- If there is no assistant message yet: show an error (“No agent messages to copy yet.” or equivalent).
- On success: show a short status (“Copied …”).
- On failure: show an actionable error (clipboard unavailable).

- Avoid feature-gating the entire command away; at minimum provide a runtime fallback that prints the text or writes it to a temp file with a clear message.

 {"id":"bd-29c","title":"Workstream: popular extension sample selection + acquisition complete","description":"Purpose:
- Produce a *large, diverse, popular* extension sample with frozen versions and archived artifacts so conformance tests are repeatable.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Sample selection criteria + sampling matrix (documented).
- Final manifest of chosen extensions (name, source, version, checksum, capabilities, categories).
- Archived artifacts (tarballs/zip) stored under a dedicated fixtures area with checksums.

Definition of done:
- Sample list is final and frozen with versions.
- Artifacts are locally archived with checksums.

-{"id":"bd-346","title":"Workstream: Session Index Integration","description":"# Workstream: Session Index Integration

## Goal
Wire the existing SQLite session index (src/session_index.rs) into the CLI flow
for fast session discovery and resumption.

## Background
The session index module is fully implemented (360+ lines) but not used:
- SQLite-based index with WAL mode
- Fast lookups by path or CWD
- Session metadata extraction (message count, name, file size, mtime)
- Atomic operations with file locking

Currently, session operations scan the filesystem directly, which is slow
for users with many sessions.

## Current State

### What Exists (src/session_index.rs)
```rust
pub struct SessionIndex { db: SqliteConnection }

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self>
    pub fn index_session(&self, session_path: &Path) -> Result<()>
    pub fn list_sessions(&self, cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn find_recent(&self, cwd: &str) -> Result<Option<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn reindex_all(&self) -> Result<()>
}

pub struct SessionMeta {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub cwd: String,
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub message_count: u32,
    pub created_at: DateTime,
    pub modified_at: DateTime,
    pub file_size: u64,
}
```

### What's Missing
1. No calls to `index_session()` after session.persist()
2. No calls to `list_sessions()` in session picker
3. No calls to `find_recent()` for --continue flag
4. No index maintenance hooks

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Wire Index on Session Save
- After session.persist(), call session_index.index_session()
- Index path stored at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/index.db

### Phase 2: Use Index for Session Listing
- --resume flag queries index instead of filesystem walk
- Session picker uses indexed metadata
- Much faster for users with 100+ sessions

### Phase 3: Use Index for --continue
- --continue queries index for most recent by CWD
- Falls back to filesystem if index empty/stale

### Phase 4: Index Maintenance
- Background reindex on startup (if stale)
- Handle deleted sessions (prune index)
- Handle moved sessions (update paths)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Sessions indexed on save
- [ ] Session picker uses index
- [ ] --continue uses index
- [ ] Startup remains fast (<100ms)
- [ ] Handles index corruption gracefully

## Files Affected
- src/session_index.rs (already implemented)
- src/session.rs (add indexing calls)
- src/session_picker.rs (use index queries)
- src/main.rs (wire up index)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.943589682Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.950957211Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-346","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.950924399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9sa","title":"Implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper","description":"# Implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper

## Goal
Create a functional HTTP client in src/http/client.rs using asupersync APIs.

## Current State
src/http/client.rs has:
- Client struct (empty)
- RequestBuilder struct with method/url/headers/body
- RequestBuilder::send() returns Err("asupersync HTTP client not yet implemented")
- RequestBuilder::json() silently ignores serialization errors

## Implementation Requirements

### Client API
```rust
use asupersync::{Cx, http::client::HttpClient};
use asupersync::tls::TlsConnectorBuilder;

pub struct Client {
    inner: HttpClient,
}

impl Client {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Create TLS connector with native roots
        let tls = TlsConnectorBuilder::new()
            .with_native_roots()?
            .build()?;
        let inner = HttpClient::new(tls);
        Ok(Self { inner })
    }
    
    pub fn request(&self, method: Method, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder {
        RequestBuilder::new(self, method, url)
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder { ... }
    pub fn post(&self, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder { ... }
}
```

### RequestBuilder API
```rust
impl RequestBuilder {
    pub fn header(mut self, key: &str, value: &str) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn json<T: Serialize>(mut self, body: &T) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn body(mut self, bytes: Vec<u8>) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn timeout(mut self, duration: Duration) -> Self { ... }
    
    pub async fn send(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Response, Error> {
        // Actually send the request using asupersync
    }
}
```

### Response API
```rust
pub struct Response {
    status: StatusCode,
    headers: Headers,
    body: BodyReader,  // Async streaming body
}

impl Response {
    pub fn status(&self) -> StatusCode { ... }
    pub fn headers(&self) -> &Headers { ... }
    pub async fn text(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<String, Error> { ... }
    pub async fn json<T: DeserializeOwned>(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<T, Error> { ... }
    pub fn bytes_stream(self) -> impl Stream<Item = Result<Bytes, Error>> { ... }
}
```

## Key Considerations
1. **TLS**: Use rustls with native root certificates (no openssl)
2. **Timeout**: Support per-request timeout via asupersync Budget
3. **Streaming**: Response body must support async streaming for SSE
4. **Headers**: Support standard headers (Content-Type, Authorization, etc.)
5. **Error handling**: Wrap asupersync errors in pi's Error type

## Testing
- Unit test: Client creation succeeds
- Unit test: RequestBuilder builds correct request
- Integration test: GET request to httpbin.org/get
- Integration test: POST with JSON body
- Integration test: Timeout behavior

## Files
- src/http/client.rs (modify)
- src/http/mod.rs (update re-exports)
- src/error.rs (add HTTP error variants)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Client::new() creates working HTTP client
- [ ] GET/POST requests work
- [ ] JSON body serialization works
- [ ] Response streaming works
- [ ] Timeout respected
- [ ] TLS verification works
- [ ] All unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:01.120130883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:12:08.576779013Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:12:08.576706438Z","close_reason":"Completed (HTTP client wrapper is implemented + tests pass; see comments)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9sa","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:01.127860687Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-9sa","author":"AmberForest","text":"Taking this bead: implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper (no tokio/reqwest).  {"id":"bd-ah1","title":"Implement session persistence cycle tests","description":"# Implement session persistence cycle tests

## Goal
Test the full session lifecycle: create → save → reload → verify integrity, with detailed logs.

## Background
Session persistence involves:
- JSONL file format (one JSON object per line)
- Header with metadata (version, cwd, timestamp)
- Message entries with parent/child relationships
- Tree structure for conversation branching
- File locking for concurrent access

Current tests cover serialization but not full I/O cycles.

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- Use temp dirs and deterministic paths; avoid asserting exact timestamps.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log session path, header fields, message IDs, tree shape, and file sizes.
- On failure, dump the JSONL contents (redacted) and parsed header.

## Test Categories

### 1. Basic Persistence Cycle Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_session_save_and_reload() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project/path");
    session.add_user_message("Hello");
    session.add_assistant_message("Hi there!");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.messages().len(), 2);
}
```

### 2. Tree Structure Persistence Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_branching_persists() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project");
    let root_id = session.add_user_message("Root");
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch A");

    session.navigate_to(root_id.clone());
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch B");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    let children = loaded.children_of(&root_id);
    assert_eq!(children.len(), 2);
}
```

### 3. Header Metadata Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_header_metadata_preserved() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/my/project/path");
    session.set_provider("anthropic");
    session.set_model("claude-opus-4");
    session.set_thinking_level(ThinkingLevel::High);
    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.cwd(), "/my/project/path");
}
```

### 4. Entry Type Persistence Tests
- ModelChange entries
- ThinkingLevel entries
- Compaction entries
- Tool invocation entries (if serialized)

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Missing file
- Corrupted JSONL
- Partial corruption (valid header + bad line)

### 6. Concurrent Access Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_concurrent_writes_locked() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let session1 = Session::new("/project");
    session1.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle1 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 1");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle2 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 2");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let (r1, r2) = join(handle1, handle2);
    assert!(r1.unwrap().is_ok() || r2.unwrap().is_ok());
}
```

### 7. Directory Structure Tests
- Persist creates nested directories when needed

## Dependencies
None (uses existing session module)

## Files
- tests/session_persistence.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ session persistence tests
- [ ] Save/reload cycle tested
- [ ] Tree structure preserved
- [ ] All entry types tested
- [ ] Error handling tested
- [ ] Concurrent access tested
- [ ] Logs include paths + header fields
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"claude-opus","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:00.745045518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:49:20.977095130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:49:20.977021223Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ah1","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:00.753607302Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ah1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:44:12.670095866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-ah1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session persistence cycle tests implementation complete.

 {"id":"bd-axuu","title":"Workstream: /settings UI Parity + config persistence","description":"# Goal
Bring `/settings` and config editing UX to parity with legacy pi-mono:
- `/settings` opens an in-app UI for common options.
- Changes persist to JSON settings files with correct precedence (global vs project).

# Legacy Spec
- Settings docs: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md`
- `/settings` is listed in interactive command table in legacy README.

Legacy `/settings` covers (at least):
- thinking level
- theme
- message delivery (steering/follow-up)
- model cycling scope

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `/settings` prints a JSON summary only; no editor UI.
- Several config fields exist in `src/config.rs` but are not wired into the interactive UX.

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Implement a `/settings` UI component:
   - select/toggle settings
   - show current value
   - confirm/apply
2) Persist changes:
   - write to `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` (global) and/or `.pi/settings.json` (project)
   - follow existing merge semantics from `src/config.rs`
3) Ensure changes take effect without restart where possible.

# Notes
- Avoid editing raw JSON in the TUI for now; prefer focused selectors.
- For settings that depend on other workstreams (themes, keybindings), include partial functionality but keep UX consistent.

# Testing
- Unit tests for persistence and merge semantics.
- State tests for `/settings` selection flows.

# Dependencies
- Keybinding workstream (`bd-3ip`) for consistent navigation.
- Theme system workstream (`bd-22p`) for actual theme switching.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] /settings opens interactive UI and restores editor
[ ] Changes persist to global/project settings
[ ] Message delivery settings configurable
[ ] Theme selection supported (once theme system exists)
[ ] Display/editor toggles configurable
